sandbox_vpc.isolation_rules: No peering, no transit gateway, NAT for outbound only

resources.name: ambersquid-victim-iam-user
resources.purpose: Over-permissioned IAM user whose long-lived access key is injected into ECS task environment variables, simulating the credential handoff that drives the entire AMBERSQUID kill chain (T1204.003, T1078.004)
resources.configuration_notes: No console access. Inline policy ambersquid-victim-policy attached. Access key output as Pulumi stack secret. Key ID and secret are injected verbatim into ambersquid-task-definition container environment as AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.

resources.name: ambersquid-victim-access-key
resources.purpose: Long-lived access key for victim IAM user — passed as plaintext env vars to ECS container, replicating how AMBERSQUID harvests credentials from the running container context (T1204.003)
resources.configuration_notes: status: Active. Export id and secret as Pulumi stack outputs with secret: true. These values are referenced by ambersquid-task-definition environment block.

resources.name: ambersquid-victim-policy
resources.purpose: INTENTIONALLY over-permissioned inline policy granting every AWS service AMBERSQUID abuses after stealing credentials — enables full emulation of T1136.003 (role creation), T1098.001 (credential manipulation), T1070 (trail deletion), and multi-service miner deployment (T1610, T1578.002 simulated). WHY over-permissioned: AMBERSQUID obtains victim creds and immediately creates AdministratorAccess-equivalent roles (ecsTaskExecutionRole) and deploys to 8+ services across 16 regions.
resources.configuration_notes: Attach inline to ambersquid-victim-iam-user. See iam_policies section for full document. Grants iam:CreateRole/AttachRolePolicy/PassRole, ecs:*, sagemaker:*, codecommit:*, codebuild:*, amplify:*, cloudformation:*, autoscaling:*, imagebuilder:*, glue:*, ecr:GetAuthorizationToken, cloudtrail:StopLogging/DeleteTrail, s3:DeleteObject, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue/ListSecrets, logs:*.

resources.name: ambersquid-ecs-cluster
resources.purpose: ECS cluster where malicious container executes with victim credentials in environment — the initial execution plane for AMBERSQUID's container-based attack (T1204.003). Service creation is NOT performed (T1610 simulated — desiredCount=0).
resources.configuration_notes: settings: [{name: containerInsights, value: enabled}]. capacityProviders: [FARGATE]. No ECS service resource created — task definition is registered only. Simulates T1610 per operational_notes (launching Fargate services with desiredCount 30 across 16 regions creates massive compute charges).

resources.name: ambersquid-task-definition
resources.purpose: ECS task definition that injects victim AWS credentials as plaintext container environment variables — the structural representation of how AMBERSQUID's malicious image receives and exercises stolen credentials (T1204.003, T1078.004). Uses benign ubuntu image as stand-in for staged miner.
resources.configuration_notes: networkMode: awsvpc. requiresCompatibilities: [FARGATE]. cpu: 256, memory: 512. Container: name=miner-container, image=ubuntu:22.04 (benign stand-in for T1608.001 staged malicious image). command: [sleep, infinity] (simulates T1496 without cost — do NOT use real SRBMiner-MULTI binary). Environment: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${ambersquid-victim-access-key.id}, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${ambersquid-victim-access-key.secret}, AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1. executionRoleArn: ambersquid-ecs-execution-role ARN. logConfiguration: awslogs, group=/ecs/ambersquid, region=us-east-1, stream-prefix=miner.

resources.name: ambersquid-ecs-execution-role
resources.purpose: Minimum-privilege ECS task execution role for image pull and log delivery — NOT the attacker-created ecsTaskExecutionRole (which is simulated via attack.py API calls, not provisioned here)
resources.configuration_notes: assumeRolePolicyDocument: {Service: ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com}. Attach only AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy managed policy. This is the legitimate infra role; the attacker-created ecsTaskExecutionRole with AdministratorAccess is created at runtime by the emulation script via iam:CreateRole calls using victim credentials.

resources.name: ambersquid-codecommit-repo
resources.purpose: CodeCommit repository named 'test' — replicates the empty repo AMBERSQUID creates per region before attempting to push miner binaries (T1525 simulated: repo created, no malicious content pushed per operational_notes). Also serves T1580 discovery.
resources.configuration_notes: repositoryName: test. description: Internal build artifacts. Repo is created empty. Do NOT push any content — T1525 is simulated because pushing miner binaries to CodeCommit requires real victim credentials and constitutes actual malicious staging.

resources.name: ambersquid-cloudtrail-bucket
resources.purpose: S3 bucket storing CloudTrail management events — primary target for AMBERSQUID's indicator removal phase (T1070: StopLogging, DeleteTrail, or DeleteObject on log files)
resources.configuration_notes: Bucket name: ambersquid-cloudtrail-logs-${accountId} (ASCII only, no special chars). versioning: enabled. serverSideEncryptionConfiguration: AES256/aws:kms. Do NOT attach a Deny on s3:DeleteObject — the emulation must be able to exercise T1070 by deleting log objects. Bucket policy: allow CloudTrail service principal to PutObject.

resources.name: ambersquid-cloudtrail
resources.purpose: Multi-region CloudTrail trail capturing all management events — detection mechanism for the entire attack chain AND direct target of T1070 (attacker calls cloudtrail:StopLogging or cloudtrail:DeleteTrail to blind defenders)
resources.configuration_notes: isMultiRegionTrail: true. includeGlobalServiceEvents: true. enableLogFileValidation: true. s3BucketName: ambersquid-cloudtrail-bucket. isLogging: true. eventSelectors: management events ReadWriteType=All. The trail is the authoritative detection source AND the T1070 target — emulation script calls cloudtrail:StopLogging using victim credentials.

resources.name: ambersquid-terraform-state-bucket
resources.purpose: S3 bucket containing a realistic terraform.tfstate object with embedded decoy AWS credentials — honeypot discovered during T1580 enumeration, realistic bait for opportunistic credential harvesting
resources.configuration_notes: Bucket name: prod-infra-terraform-state-${accountId}. ACL: private but readable by ambersquid-victim-iam-user via s3:GetObject. Upload a terraform.tfstate object at key terraform.tfstate containing fake embedded AKIA access keys and plausible-looking secret values (use canarytokens.org or static canary strings). Realistic bucket name triggers AMBERSQUID-style opportunistic s3:ListBuckets discovery.

resources.name: ambersquid-honey-iam-user
resources.purpose: Decoy IAM user 'prod-deploy-svc' with an active but policy-less access key — its use triggers AuthFailure CloudWatch alarm, surfacing T1580 discovery and T1098.001 credential enumeration attempts
resources.configuration_notes: userName: prod-deploy-svc. Tags: Environment=production, Team=platform, ManagedBy=terraform. Create one AccessKey (Status: Active) with no attached policies. Any API call using this key returns AuthFailure and generates a CloudTrail event that can be alarmed on. The key ID and secret should be stored in ambersquid-terraform-state-bucket's fake tfstate to complete the bait chain.

resources.name: ambersquid-canary-secret
resources.purpose: SecretsManager secret named 'prod/database/master_credentials' — realistic discovery target during T1580 enumeration, surfaces attacker's secretsmanager:ListSecrets and GetSecretValue calls in CloudTrail
resources.configuration_notes: name: prod/database/master_credentials. description: RDS master credentials for production cluster. secretString: JSON with keys username, password, host, port using plausible-looking but entirely fake values (e.g., host=prod-db-cluster.cluster-xxxx.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com). Tags: Environment=production, Team=data. Accessible via secretsmanager:GetSecretValue by ambersquid-victim-iam-user.

resources.name: ambersquid-vpc
resources.purpose: Isolated lab VPC — enforces network boundary for ECS Fargate tasks, prevents mining pool egress at network layer
resources.configuration_notes: cidrBlock: 10.99.0.0/16. enableDnsHostnames: true. enableDnsSupport: true. No VPC peering. No transit gateway.

resources.name: ambersquid-public-subnet
resources.purpose: Public subnet for ECS Fargate task placement — requires internet route for AWS API calls made by the simulated miner using victim credentials
resources.configuration_notes: cidrBlock: 10.99.1.0/24. availabilityZone: us-east-1a. mapPublicIpOnLaunch: true. Associate with ambersquid-igw route table (0.0.0.0/0 → IGW).

resources.name: ambersquid-igw
resources.purpose: Internet gateway providing outbound AWS API access for ECS tasks
resources.configuration_notes: Attach to ambersquid-vpc. Create route table with 0.0.0.0/0 → ambersquid-igw and associate with ambersquid-public-subnet.

resources.name: ambersquid-task-sg
resources.purpose: Security group for ECS tasks — allows only HTTPS egress for AWS API calls; explicitly blocks all known cryptomining pool ports to prevent T1496 from materializing even if emulation configuration changes
resources.configuration_notes: vpcId: ambersquid-vpc. Ingress: none. Egress rules: Allow TCP/443 to 0.0.0.0/0 (AWS API endpoint access). Deny (no rule = implicit deny) all of: TCP 3333, 4444, 5555, 7777, 8888, 9999, 14444, 45560 (common mining pool ports). This allows AWS SDK calls while preventing actual resource hijacking per T1496 operational note.

resources.name: ambersquid-log-group
resources.purpose: CloudWatch log group for ECS container output and emulation event capture

iam_policies.name: ambersquid-victim-policy
iam_policies.rationale: INTENTIONALLY over-permissioned. AMBERSQUID obtains these credentials via malicious container env vars (T1204.003) then immediately creates AdministratorAccess-equivalent IAM roles (T1136.003) and deploys miners to ECS, SageMaker, CodeBuild, Amplify, CloudFormation, Auto Scaling, ImageBuilder, Glue (T1610/T1578.002 simulated). Indicator removal (T1070) requires cloudtrail:StopLogging and s3:DeleteObject.
